You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines repository.
The codebase is at version 1.7 (commit: 2653cd23).

## Issue to Resolve

`SharedFlow.toList(mutableList)`: difference behavior between 1.6.4 vs 1.7.0-Beta
Sometimes, I use this method to write simple test on SharedFlow.
Version 1.6.4 works well.
Since 1.7.0-Beta, this method is deprecated and the implementation seem be wrong

```kotlin
val acc = MutableList()
val job = scope.launch(start = Undispatched) {
  sharedFlow.toList(acc)
}

// later...

job.cancel()
assertContentEquals(
 expected = ...,
 actual = acc
)
```

IMO, the correct impl. is `SharedFlow.toList(acc) = (this as Flow).toList(acc)`

  5â†’package kotlinx.coroutines.flow
  6â†’
  7â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.*
  8â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.channels.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.internal.*
 10â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.internal.*
 11â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
 12â†’import kotlin.jvm.*
 13â†’
 14â†’/**
 15â†’ * A _hot_ [Flow] that shares emitted values among all its collectors in a broadcast fashion, so that all collectors
 16â†’ * get all emitted values. A shared flow is called _hot_ because its active instance exists independently of the
 17â†’ * presence of collectors. This is opposed to a regular [Flow], such as defined by the [`flow { ... }`][flow] function,
 18â†’ * which is _cold_ and is started separately for each collector.
 19â†’ *
 20â†’ * **Shared flow never completes**. A call to [Flow.collect] on a shared flow never completes normally, and
 21â†’ * neither does a coroutine started by the [Flow.launchIn] function. An active collector of a shared flow is called a _subscriber_.
 22â†’ *
 23â†’ * A subscriber of a shared flow can be cancelled. This usually happens when the scope in which the coroutine is running
 24â†’ * is cancelled. A subscriber to a shared flow is always [cancellable][Flow.cancellable], and checks for
 25â†’ * cancellation before each emission. Note that most terminal operators like [Flow.toList] would also not complete,
 26â†’ * when applied to a shared flow, but flow-truncating operators like [Flow.take] and [Flow.takeWhile] can be used on a
 27â†’ * shared flow to turn it into a completing one.
 28â†’ *
 29â†’ * A [mutable shared flow][MutableSharedFlow] is created using the [MutableSharedFlow(...)] constructor function.
 30â†’ * Its state can be updated by [emitting][MutableSharedFlow.emit] values to it and performing other operations.
 31â†’ * See the [MutableSharedFlow] documentation for details.
 32â†’ *
 33â†’ * [SharedFlow] is useful for broadcasting events that happen inside an application to subscribers that can come and go.
 34â†’ * For example, the following class encapsulates an event bus that distributes events to all subscribers
 35â†’ * in a _rendezvous_ manner, suspending until all subscribers receive emitted event:
 36â†’ *
 37â†’ * ```
 38â†’ * class EventBus {
 39â†’ *     private val _events = MutableSharedFlow<Event>() // private mutable shared flow
 40â†’ *     val events = _events.asSharedFlow() // publicly exposed as read-only shared flow
 41â†’ *
 42â†’ *     suspend fun produceEvent(event: Event) {
 43â†’ *         _events.emit(event) // suspends until all subscribers receive it
 44â†’ *     }
 45â†’ * }
 46â†’ * ```
 47â†’ *
 48â†’ * As an alternative to the above usage with the `MutableSharedFlow(...)` constructor function,
 49â†’ * any _cold_ [Flow] can be converted to a shared flow using the [shareIn] operator.
 50â†’ *
 51â†’ * There is a specialized implementation of shared flow for the case where the most recent state value needs
 52â†’ * to be shared. See [StateFlow] for details.
 53â†’ *
 54â†’ * ### Replay cache and buffer
 55â†’ *
 56â†’ * A shared flow keeps a specific number of the most recent values in its _replay cache_. Every new subscriber first
 57â†’ * gets the values from the replay cache and then gets new emitted values. The maximum size of the replay cache is
 58â†’ * specified when the shared flow is created by the `replay` parameter. A snapshot of the current replay cache
 59â†’ * is available via the [replayCache] property and it can be reset with the [MutableSharedFlow.resetReplayCache] function.
 60â†’ *
 61â†’ * A replay cache also provides buffer for emissions to the shared flow, allowing slow subscribers to
 62â†’ * get values from the buffer without suspending emitters. The buffer space determines how much slow subscribers
 63â†’ * can lag from the fast ones. When creating a shared flow, additional buffer capacity beyond replay can be reserved
 64â†’ * using the `extraBufferCapacity` parameter.
 65â†’ * 
 66â†’ * A shared flow with a buffer can be configured to avoid suspension of emitters on buffer overflow using
 67â†’ * the `onBufferOverflow` parameter, which is equal to one of the entries of the [BufferOverflow] enum. When a strategy other
 68â†’ * than [SUSPENDED][BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] is configured, emissions to the shared flow never suspend.
 69â†’ *
 70â†’ * **Buffer overflow condition can happen only when there is at least one subscriber that is not ready to accept
 71â†’ * the new value.**  In the absence of subscribers only the most recent `replay` values are stored and the buffer
 72â†’ * overflow behavior is never triggered and has no effect. In particular, in the absence of subscribers emitter never
 73â†’ * suspends despite [BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] option and [BufferOverflow.DROP_LATEST] option does not have effect either.
 74â†’ * Essentially, the behavior in the absence of subscribers is always similar to [BufferOverflow.DROP_OLDEST],
 75â†’ * but the buffer is just of `replay` size (without any `extraBufferCapacity`).
 76â†’ *
 77â†’ * ### Unbuffered shared flow
 78â†’ *
 79â†’ * A default implementation of a shared flow that is created with `MutableSharedFlow()` constructor function
 80â†’ * without parameters has no replay cache nor additional buffer.
 81â†’ * [emit][MutableSharedFlow.emit] call to such a shared flow suspends until all subscribers receive the emitted value
 82â†’ * and returns immediately if there are no subscribers.
 83â†’ * Thus, [tryEmit][MutableSharedFlow.tryEmit] call succeeds and returns `true` only if
 84â†’ * there are no subscribers (in which case the emitted value is immediately lost).
 85â†’ *
 86â†’ * ### SharedFlow vs BroadcastChannel
 87â†’ *
 88â†’ * Conceptually shared flow is similar to [BroadcastChannel][BroadcastChannel]
 89â†’ * and is designed to completely replace it.
 90â†’ * It has the following important differences:
 91â†’ *
 92â†’ * * `SharedFlow` is simpler, because it does not have to implement all the [Channel] APIs, which allows
 93â†’ *    for faster and simpler implementation.
 94â†’ * * `SharedFlow` supports configurable replay and buffer overflow strategy.
 95â†’ * * `SharedFlow` has a clear separation into a read-only `SharedFlow` interface and a [MutableSharedFlow].
 96â†’ * * `SharedFlow` cannot be closed like `BroadcastChannel` and can never represent a failure.
 97â†’ *    All errors and completion signals should be explicitly _materialized_ if needed.
 98â†’ *
 99â†’ * To migrate [BroadcastChannel] usage to [SharedFlow], start by replacing usages of the `BroadcastChannel(capacity)`
100â†’ * constructor with `MutableSharedFlow(0, extraBufferCapacity=capacity)` (broadcast channel does not replay
101â†’ * values to new subscribers). Replace [send][BroadcastChannel.send] and [trySend][BroadcastChannel.trySend] calls
102â†’ * with [emit][MutableStateFlow.emit] and [tryEmit][MutableStateFlow.tryEmit], and convert subscribers' code to flow operators.
103â†’ *
104â†’ * ### Concurrency
105â†’ *
106â†’ * All methods of shared flow are **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
107â†’ * external synchronization.
108â†’ *
109â†’ * ### Operator fusion
110â†’ *
111â†’ * Application of [flowOn][Flow.flowOn], [buffer] with [RENDEZVOUS][Channel.RENDEZVOUS] capacity,
112â†’ * or [cancellable] operators to a shared flow has no effect.
113â†’ *
114â†’ * ### Implementation notes
115â†’ *
116â†’ * Shared flow implementation uses a lock to ensure thread-safety, but suspending collector and emitter coroutines are
117â†’ * resumed outside of this lock to avoid deadlocks when using unconfined coroutines. Adding new subscribers
118â†’ * has `O(1)` amortized cost, but emitting has `O(N)` cost, where `N` is the number of subscribers.
119â†’ *
120â†’ * ### Not stable for inheritance
121â†’ *
122â†’ * **The `SharedFlow` interface is not stable for inheritance in 3rd party libraries**, as new methods
123â†’ * might be added to this interface in the future, but is stable for use.
124â†’ * Use the `MutableSharedFlow(replay, ...)` constructor function to create an implementation.
125â†’ */
126â†’public interface SharedFlow<out T> : Flow<T> {
127â†’    /**
128â†’     * A snapshot of the replay cache.
129â†’     */
130â†’    public val replayCache: List<T>
131â†’
132â†’    /**
133â†’     * Accepts the given [collector] and [emits][FlowCollector.emit] values into it.
134â†’     * To emit values from a shared flow into a specific collector, either `collector.emitAll(flow)` or `collect { ... }`
135â†’     * SAM-conversion can be used.
136â†’     *
137â†’     * **A shared flow never completes**. A call to [Flow.collect] or any other terminal operator
138â†’     * on a shared flow never completes normally.
139â†’     *
140â†’     * @see [Flow.collect] for implementation and inheritance details.
141â†’     */
142â†’    override suspend fun collect(collector: FlowCollector<T>): Nothing
143â†’}
144â†’
145â†’/**
146â†’ * A mutable [SharedFlow] that provides functions to [emit] values to the flow.
147â†’ * An instance of `MutableSharedFlow` with the given configuration parameters can be created using `MutableSharedFlow(...)`
148â†’ * constructor function.
149â†’ *
150â†’ * See the [SharedFlow] documentation for details on shared flows.
151â†’ *
152â†’ * `MutableSharedFlow` is a [SharedFlow] that also provides the abilities to [emit] a value,
153â†’ * to [tryEmit] without suspension if possible, to track the [subscriptionCount],
154â†’ * and to [resetReplayCache].
155â†’ *
156â†’ * ### Concurrency
157â†’ *
158â†’ * All methods of shared flow are **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
159â†’ * external synchronization.
160â†’ *
161â†’ * ### Not stable for inheritance
162â†’ *
163â†’ * **The `MutableSharedFlow` interface is not stable for inheritance in 3rd party libraries**, as new methods
164â†’ * might be added to this interface in the future, but is stable for use.
165â†’ * Use the `MutableSharedFlow(...)` constructor function to create an implementation.
166â†’ */
167â†’public interface MutableSharedFlow<T> : SharedFlow<T>, FlowCollector<T> {
168â†’    /**
169â†’     * Emits a [value] to this shared flow, suspending on buffer overflow.
170â†’     *
171â†’     * This call can suspend only when the [BufferOverflow] strategy is
172â†’     * [SUSPEND][BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] **and** there are subscribers collecting this shared flow.
173â†’     *
174â†’     * If there are no subscribers, the buffer is not used.
175â†’     * Instead, the most recently emitted value is simply stored into
176â†’     * the replay cache if one was configured, displacing the older elements there,
177â†’     * or dropped if no replay cache was configured.
178â†’     *
179â†’     * See [tryEmit] for a non-suspending variant of this function.
180â†’     *
181â†’     * This method is **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
182â†’     * external synchronization.
183â†’     */
184â†’    override suspend fun emit(value: T)
185â†’
186â†’    /**
187â†’     * Tries to emit a [value] to this shared flow without suspending. It returns `true` if the value was
188â†’     * emitted successfully (see below). When this function returns `false`, it means that a call to a plain [emit]
189â†’     * function would suspend until there is buffer space available.
190â†’     *
191â†’     * This call can return `false` only when the [BufferOverflow] strategy is
192â†’     * [SUSPEND][BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] **and** there are subscribers collecting this shared flow.
193â†’     *
194â†’     * If there are no subscribers, the buffer is not used.
195â†’     * Instead, the most recently emitted value is simply stored into
196â†’     * the replay cache if one was configured, displacing the older elements there,
197â†’     * or dropped if no replay cache was configured. In any case, `tryEmit` returns `true`.
198â†’     *
199â†’     * This method is **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
200â†’     * external synchronization.
201â†’     */
202â†’    public fun tryEmit(value: T): Boolean
203â†’
204â†’    /**
205â†’     * The number of subscribers (active collectors) to this shared flow.
206â†’     *
207â†’     * The integer in the resulting [StateFlow] is not negative and starts with zero for a freshly created
208â†’     * shared flow.
209â†’     *
210â†’     * This state can be used to react to changes in the number of subscriptions to this shared flow.
211â†’     * For example, if you need to call `onActive` when the first subscriber appears and `onInactive`
212â†’     * when the last one disappears, you can set it up like this:
213â†’     *
214â†’     * ```
215â†’     * sharedFlow.subscriptionCount
216â†’     *     .map { count -> count > 0 } // map count into active/inactive flag
217â†’     *     .distinctUntilChanged() // only react to true<->false changes
218â†’     *     .onEach { isActive -> // configure an action
219â†’     *         if (isActive) onActive() else onInactive()
220â†’     *     }
221â†’     *     .launchIn(scope) // launch it
222â†’     * ```
223â†’     *
224â†’     * Implementation note: the resulting flow **does not** conflate subscription count.
225â†’     */
226â†’    public val subscriptionCount: StateFlow<Int>
227â†’
228â†’    /**
229â†’     * Resets the [replayCache] of this shared flow to an empty state.
230â†’     * New subscribers will be receiving only the values that were emitted after this call,
231â†’     * while old subscribers will still be receiving previously buffered values.
232â†’     * To reset a shared flow to an initial value, emit the value after this call.
233â†’     *
234â†’     * On a [MutableStateFlow], which always contains a single value, this function is not
235â†’     * supported, and throws an [UnsupportedOperationException]. To reset a [MutableStateFlow]
236â†’     * to an initial value, just update its [value][MutableStateFlow.value].
237â†’     *
238â†’     * This method is **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
239â†’     * external synchronization.
240â†’     *
241â†’     * **Note: This is an experimental api.** This function may be removed or renamed in the future.
242â†’     */
243â†’    @ExperimentalCoroutinesApi
244â†’    public fun resetReplayCache()
245â†’}
246â†’
247â†’/**
248â†’ * Creates a [MutableSharedFlow] with the given configuration parameters.
249â†’ *
250â†’ * This function throws [IllegalArgumentException] on unsupported values of parameters or combinations thereof.
251â†’ *
252â†’ * @param replay the number of values replayed to new subscribers (cannot be negative, defaults to zero).
253â†’ * @param extraBufferCapacity the number of values buffered in addition to `replay`.
254â†’ *   [emit][MutableSharedFlow.emit] does not suspend while there is a buffer space remaining (optional, cannot be negative, defaults to zero).
255â†’ * @param onBufferOverflow configures an [emit][MutableSharedFlow.emit] action on buffer overflow. Optional, defaults to
256â†’ *   [suspending][BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] attempts to emit a value.
257â†’ *   Values other than [BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] are supported only when `replay > 0` or `extraBufferCapacity > 0`.
258â†’ *   **Buffer overflow can happen only when there is at least one subscriber that is not ready to accept
259â†’ *   the new value.** In the absence of subscribers only the most recent [replay] values are stored and
260â†’ *   the buffer overflow behavior is never triggered and has no effect.
261â†’ */
262â†’@Suppress("FunctionName", "UNCHECKED_CAST")
263â†’public fun <T> MutableSharedFlow(
264â†’    replay: Int = 0,
265â†’    extraBufferCapacity: Int = 0,
266â†’    onBufferOverflow: BufferOverflow = BufferOverflow.SUSPEND
267â†’): MutableSharedFlow<T> {
268â†’    require(replay >= 0) { "replay cannot be negative, but was $replay" }
269â†’    require(extraBufferCapacity >= 0) { "extraBufferCapacity cannot be negative, but was $extraBufferCapacity" }
270â†’    require(replay > 0 || extraBufferCapacity > 0 || onBufferOverflow == BufferOverflow.SUSPEND) {
271â†’        "replay or extraBufferCapacity must be positive with non-default onBufferOverflow strategy $onBufferOverflow"
272â†’    }
273â†’    val bufferCapacity0 = replay + extraBufferCapacity
274â†’    val bufferCapacity = if (bufferCapacity0 < 0) Int.MAX_VALUE else bufferCapacity0 // coerce to MAX_VALUE on overflow
275â†’    return SharedFlowImpl(replay, bufferCapacity, onBufferOverflow)
276â†’}

280â†’internal class SharedFlowSlot : AbstractSharedFlowSlot<SharedFlowImpl<*>>() {
281â†’    @JvmField
282â†’    var index = -1L // current "to-be-emitted" index, -1 means the slot is free now
283â†’
284â†’    @JvmField
285â†’    var cont: Continuation<Unit>? = null // collector waiting for new value
286â†’
287â†’    override fun allocateLocked(flow: SharedFlowImpl<*>): Boolean {
288â†’        if (index >= 0) return false // not free
289â†’        index = flow.updateNewCollectorIndexLocked()
290â†’        return true
291â†’    }
292â†’
293â†’    override fun freeLocked(flow: SharedFlowImpl<*>): Array<Continuation<Unit>?> {
294â†’        assert { index >= 0 }
295â†’        val oldIndex = index
296â†’        index = -1L
297â†’        cont = null // cleanup continuation reference
298â†’        return flow.updateCollectorIndexLocked(oldIndex)
299â†’    }
300â†’}

302â†’internal open class SharedFlowImpl<T>(
303â†’    private val replay: Int,
304â†’    private val bufferCapacity: Int,
305â†’    private val onBufferOverflow: BufferOverflow
306â†’) : AbstractSharedFlow<SharedFlowSlot>(), MutableSharedFlow<T>, CancellableFlow<T>, FusibleFlow<T> {
307â†’    /*
308â†’        Logical structure of the buffer
309â†’
310â†’                  buffered values
311â†’             /-----------------------\
312â†’                          replayCache      queued emitters
313â†’                          /----------\/----------------------\
314â†’         +---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+
315â†’         |   | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 | E | E | E | E | E | E |   |   |   |
316â†’         +---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+
317â†’               ^           ^           ^                      ^
318â†’               |           |           |                      |
319â†’              head         |      head + bufferSize     head + totalSize
320â†’               |           |           |
321â†’     index of the slowest  |    index of the fastest
322â†’      possible collector   |     possible collector
323â†’               |           |
324â†’               |     replayIndex == new collector's index
325â†’               \---------------------- /
326â†’          range of possible minCollectorIndex
327â†’
328â†’          head == minOf(minCollectorIndex, replayIndex) // by definition
329â†’          totalSize == bufferSize + queueSize // by definition
330â†’
331â†’       INVARIANTS:
332â†’          minCollectorIndex = activeSlots.minOf { it.index } ?: (head + bufferSize)
333â†’          replayIndex <= head + bufferSize
334â†’     */
335â†’
336â†’    // Stored state
337â†’    private var buffer: Array<Any?>? = null // allocated when needed, allocated size always power of two
338â†’    private var replayIndex = 0L // minimal index from which new collector gets values
339â†’    private var minCollectorIndex = 0L // minimal index of active collectors, equal to replayIndex if there are none
340â†’    private var bufferSize = 0 // number of buffered values
341â†’    private var queueSize = 0 // number of queued emitters
342â†’
343â†’    // Computed state
344â†’    private val head: Long get() = minOf(minCollectorIndex, replayIndex)
345â†’    private val replaySize: Int get() = (head + bufferSize - replayIndex).toInt()
346â†’    private val totalSize: Int get() = bufferSize + queueSize
347â†’    private val bufferEndIndex: Long get() = head + bufferSize
348â†’    private val queueEndIndex: Long get() = head + bufferSize + queueSize
349â†’
350â†’    override val replayCache: List<T>
351â†’        get() = synchronized(this) {
352â†’            val replaySize = this.replaySize
353â†’            if (replaySize == 0) return emptyList()
354â†’            val result = ArrayList<T>(replaySize)
355â†’            val buffer = buffer!! // must be allocated, because replaySize > 0
356â†’            @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
357â†’            for (i in 0 until replaySize) result += buffer.getBufferAt(replayIndex + i) as T
358â†’            result
359â†’        }
360â†’
361â†’    /*
362â†’     * A tweak for SubscriptionCountStateFlow to get the latest value.
363â†’     */
364â†’    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
365â†’    protected val lastReplayedLocked: T
366â†’        get() = buffer!!.getBufferAt(replayIndex + replaySize - 1) as T
367â†’
368â†’    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
369â†’    override suspend fun collect(collector: FlowCollector<T>): Nothing {
370â†’        val slot = allocateSlot()
371â†’        try {
372â†’            if (collector is SubscribedFlowCollector) collector.onSubscription()
373â†’            val collectorJob = currentCoroutineContext()[Job]
374â†’            while (true) {
375â†’                var newValue: Any?
376â†’                while (true) {
377â†’                    newValue = tryTakeValue(slot) // attempt no-suspend fast path first
378â†’                    if (newValue !== NO_VALUE) break
379â†’                    awaitValue(slot) // await signal that the new value is available
380â†’                }
381â†’                collectorJob?.ensureActive()
382â†’                collector.emit(newValue as T)
383â†’            }
384â†’        } finally {
385â†’            freeSlot(slot)
386â†’        }
387â†’    }
388â†’
389â†’    override fun tryEmit(value: T): Boolean {
390â†’        var resumes: Array<Continuation<Unit>?> = EMPTY_RESUMES
391â†’        val emitted = synchronized(this) {
392â†’            if (tryEmitLocked(value)) {
393â†’                resumes = findSlotsToResumeLocked(resumes)
394â†’                true
395â†’            } else {
396â†’                false
397â†’            }
398â†’        }
399â†’        for (cont in resumes) cont?.resume(Unit)
400â†’        return emitted
401â†’    }
402â†’
403â†’    override suspend fun emit(value: T) {
404â†’        if (tryEmit(value)) return // fast-path
405â†’        emitSuspend(value)
406â†’    }
407â†’
408â†’    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
409â†’    private fun tryEmitLocked(value: T): Boolean {
410â†’        // Fast path without collectors -> no buffering
411â†’        if (nCollectors == 0) return tryEmitNoCollectorsLocked(value) // always returns true
412â†’        // With collectors we'll have to buffer
413â†’        // cannot emit now if buffer is full & blocked by slow collectors
414â†’        if (bufferSize >= bufferCapacity && minCollectorIndex <= replayIndex) {
415â†’            when (onBufferOverflow) {
416â†’                BufferOverflow.SUSPEND -> return false // will suspend
417â†’                BufferOverflow.DROP_LATEST -> return true // just drop incoming
418â†’                BufferOverflow.DROP_OLDEST -> {} // force enqueue & drop oldest instead
419â†’            }
420â†’        }
421â†’        enqueueLocked(value)
422â†’        bufferSize++ // value was added to buffer
423â†’        // drop oldest from the buffer if it became more than bufferCapacity
424â†’        if (bufferSize > bufferCapacity) dropOldestLocked()
425â†’        // keep replaySize not larger that needed
426â†’        if (replaySize > replay) { // increment replayIndex by one
427â†’            updateBufferLocked(replayIndex + 1, minCollectorIndex, bufferEndIndex, queueEndIndex)
428â†’        }
429â†’        return true
430â†’    }
431â†’
432â†’    private fun tryEmitNoCollectorsLocked(value: T): Boolean {
433â†’        assert { nCollectors == 0 }
434â†’        if (replay == 0) return true // no need to replay, just forget it now
435â†’        enqueueLocked(value) // enqueue to replayCache
436â†’        bufferSize++ // value was added to buffer
437â†’        // drop oldest from the buffer if it became more than replay
438â†’        if (bufferSize > replay) dropOldestLocked()
439â†’        minCollectorIndex = head + bufferSize // a default value (max allowed)
440â†’        return true
441â†’    }
442â†’
443â†’    private fun dropOldestLocked() {
444â†’        buffer!!.setBufferAt(head, null)
445â†’        bufferSize--
446â†’        val newHead = head + 1
447â†’        if (replayIndex < newHead) replayIndex = newHead
448â†’        if (minCollectorIndex < newHead) correctCollectorIndexesOnDropOldest(newHead)
449â†’        assert { head == newHead } // since head = minOf(minCollectorIndex, replayIndex) it should have updated
450â†’    }
451â†’
452â†’    private fun correctCollectorIndexesOnDropOldest(newHead: Long) {
453â†’        forEachSlotLocked { slot ->
454â†’            @Suppress("ConvertTwoComparisonsToRangeCheck") // Bug in JS backend
455â†’            if (slot.index >= 0 && slot.index < newHead) {
456â†’                slot.index = newHead // force move it up (this collector was too slow and missed the value at its index)
457â†’            }
458â†’        }
459â†’        minCollectorIndex = newHead
460â†’    }
461â†’
462â†’    // enqueues item to buffer array, caller shall increment either bufferSize or queueSize
463â†’    private fun enqueueLocked(item: Any?) {
464â†’        val curSize = totalSize
465â†’        val buffer = when (val curBuffer = buffer) {
466â†’            null -> growBuffer(null, 0, 2)
467â†’            else -> if (curSize >= curBuffer.size) growBuffer(curBuffer, curSize,curBuffer.size * 2) else curBuffer
468â†’        }
469â†’        buffer.setBufferAt(head + curSize, item)
470â†’    }
471â†’
472â†’    private fun growBuffer(curBuffer: Array<Any?>?, curSize: Int, newSize: Int): Array<Any?> {
473â†’        check(newSize > 0) { "Buffer size overflow" }
474â†’        val newBuffer = arrayOfNulls<Any?>(newSize).also { buffer = it }
475â†’        if (curBuffer == null) return newBuffer
476â†’        val head = head
477â†’        for (i in 0 until curSize) {
478â†’            newBuffer.setBufferAt(head + i, curBuffer.getBufferAt(head + i))
479â†’        }
480â†’        return newBuffer
481â†’    }
482â†’
483â†’    private suspend fun emitSuspend(value: T) = suspendCancellableCoroutine<Unit> sc@{ cont ->
484â†’        var resumes: Array<Continuation<Unit>?> = EMPTY_RESUMES
485â†’        val emitter = synchronized(this) lock@{
486â†’            // recheck buffer under lock again (make sure it is really full)
487â†’            if (tryEmitLocked(value)) {
488â†’                cont.resume(Unit)
489â†’                resumes = findSlotsToResumeLocked(resumes)
490â†’                return@lock null
491â†’            }
492â†’            // add suspended emitter to the buffer
493â†’            Emitter(this, head + totalSize, value, cont).also {
494â†’                enqueueLocked(it)
495â†’                queueSize++ // added to queue of waiting emitters
496â†’                // synchronous shared flow might rendezvous with waiting emitter
497â†’                if (bufferCapacity == 0) resumes = findSlotsToResumeLocked(resumes)
498â†’            }
499â†’        }
500â†’        // outside of the lock: register dispose on cancellation
501â†’        emitter?.let { cont.disposeOnCancellation(it) }
502â†’        // outside of the lock: resume slots if needed
503â†’        for (r in resumes) r?.resume(Unit)
504â†’    }
505â†’
506â†’    private fun cancelEmitter(emitter: Emitter) = synchronized(this) {
507â†’        if (emitter.index < head) return // already skipped past this index
508â†’        val buffer = buffer!!
509â†’        if (buffer.getBufferAt(emitter.index) !== emitter) return // already resumed
510â†’        buffer.setBufferAt(emitter.index, NO_VALUE)
511â†’        cleanupTailLocked()
512â†’    }
513â†’
514â†’    internal fun updateNewCollectorIndexLocked(): Long {
515â†’        val index = replayIndex
516â†’        if (index < minCollectorIndex) minCollectorIndex = index
517â†’        return index
518â†’    }
519â†’
520â†’    // Is called when a collector disappears or changes index, returns a list of continuations to resume after lock
521â†’    internal fun updateCollectorIndexLocked(oldIndex: Long): Array<Continuation<Unit>?> {
522â†’        assert { oldIndex >= minCollectorIndex }
523â†’        if (oldIndex > minCollectorIndex) return EMPTY_RESUMES // nothing changes, it was not min
524â†’        // start computing new minimal index of active collectors
525â†’        val head = head
526â†’        var newMinCollectorIndex = head + bufferSize
527â†’        // take into account a special case of sync shared flow that can go past 1st queued emitter
528â†’        if (bufferCapacity == 0 && queueSize > 0) newMinCollectorIndex++

530â†’            @Suppress("ConvertTwoComparisonsToRangeCheck") // Bug in JS backend
531â†’            if (slot.index >= 0 && slot.index < newMinCollectorIndex) newMinCollectorIndex = slot.index
532â†’        }

534â†’        if (newMinCollectorIndex <= minCollectorIndex) return EMPTY_RESUMES // nothing changes

536â†’        // We must keep all the items from newMinIndex to the end of buffer
537â†’        var newBufferEndIndex = bufferEndIndex // var to grow when waiters are resumed
538â†’        val maxResumeCount = if (nCollectors > 0) {

540â†’            // a) queueSize -> that's how many waiting emitters we have
541â†’            // b) bufferCapacity - newBufferSize0 -> that's how many we can afford to resume to add w/o exceeding bufferCapacity
542â†’            val newBufferSize0 = (newBufferEndIndex - newMinCollectorIndex).toInt()
543â†’            minOf(queueSize, bufferCapacity - newBufferSize0)

546â†’            queueSize // that's how many waiting emitters we have (at most)
547â†’        }

549â†’        val newQueueEndIndex = newBufferEndIndex + queueSize
550â†’        if (maxResumeCount > 0) { // collect emitters to resume if we have them
551â†’            resumes = arrayOfNulls(maxResumeCount)
552â†’            var resumeCount = 0
553â†’            val buffer = buffer!!
554â†’            for (curEmitterIndex in newBufferEndIndex until newQueueEndIndex) {
555â†’                val emitter = buffer.getBufferAt(curEmitterIndex)
556â†’                if (emitter !== NO_VALUE) {

567â†’        val newBufferSize1 = (newBufferEndIndex - head).toInt()

569â†’        // and newMinCollectorIndex might pointing the wrong place because of that. The easiest way to fix it is by
570â†’        // forcing newMinCollectorIndex = newBufferEndIndex. We do not needed to update newBufferSize1 (which could be
571â†’        // too big), because the only use of newBufferSize1 in the below code is in the minOf(replay, newBufferSize1)
572â†’        // expression, which coerces values that are too big anyway.

584â†’        cleanupTailLocked()

586â†’        if (resumes.isNotEmpty()) resumes = findSlotsToResumeLocked(resumes)

591â†’        newReplayIndex: Long,
592â†’        newMinCollectorIndex: Long,
593â†’        newBufferEndIndex: Long,
594â†’        newQueueEndIndex: Long

605â†’        queueSize = (newQueueEndIndex - newBufferEndIndex).toInt()

608â†’        assert { queueSize >= 0 }

613â†’    private fun cleanupTailLocked() {
614â†’        // If we have synchronous case, then keep one emitter queued
615â†’        if (bufferCapacity == 0 && queueSize <= 1) return // return, don't clear it
616â†’        val buffer = buffer!!
617â†’        while (queueSize > 0 && buffer.getBufferAt(head + totalSize - 1) === NO_VALUE) {
618â†’            queueSize--

624â†’    private fun tryTakeValue(slot: SharedFlowSlot): Any? {
625â†’        var resumes: Array<Continuation<Unit>?> = EMPTY_RESUMES

627â†’            val index = tryPeekLocked(slot)
628â†’            if (index < 0) {
629â†’                NO_VALUE

642â†’    // returns -1 if cannot peek value without suspension
643â†’    private fun tryPeekLocked(slot: SharedFlowSlot): Long {
644â†’        // return buffered value if possible
645â†’        val index = slot.index

648â†’        // Synchronous shared flow (bufferCapacity == 0) tries to rendezvous
649â†’        if (index > head) return -1L // ... but only with the first emitter (never look forward)
650â†’        if (queueSize == 0) return -1L // nothing there to rendezvous with

655â†’        when (val item = buffer!!.getBufferAt(index)) {
656â†’            is Emitter -> item.value

661â†’        synchronized(this) lock@{
662â†’            val index = tryPeekLocked(slot) // recheck under this lock
663â†’            if (index < 0) {

665â†’            } else {

  5â†’package kotlinx.coroutines.flow
  6â†’
  7â†’import kotlinx.atomicfu.*
  8â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.channels.*
 10â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.internal.*
 11â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.internal.*
 12â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
 13â†’
 14â†’/**
 15â†’ * A [SharedFlow] that represents a read-only state with a single updatable data [value] that emits updates
 16â†’ * to the value to its collectors. A state flow is a _hot_ flow because its active instance exists independently
 17â†’ * of the presence of collectors. Its current value can be retrieved via the [value] property.
 18â†’ *
 19â†’ * **State flow never completes**. A call to [Flow.collect] on a state flow never completes normally, and
 20â†’ * neither does a coroutine started by the [Flow.launchIn] function. An active collector of a state flow is called a _subscriber_.
 21â†’ *
 22â†’ * A [mutable state flow][MutableStateFlow] is created using `MutableStateFlow(value)` constructor function with
 23â†’ * the initial value. The value of mutable state flow can be updated by setting its [value] property.
 24â†’ * Updates to the [value] are always [conflated][Flow.conflate]. So a slow collector skips fast updates,
 25â†’ * but always collects the most recently emitted value.
 26â†’ *
 27â†’ * [StateFlow] is useful as a data-model class to represent any kind of state.
 28â†’ * Derived values can be defined using various operators on the flows, with [combine] operator being especially
 29â†’ * useful to combine values from multiple state flows using arbitrary functions.
 30â†’ *
 31â†’ * For example, the following class encapsulates an integer state and increments its value on each call to `inc`:
 32â†’ *
 33â†’ * ```
 34â†’ * class CounterModel {
 35â†’ *     private val _counter = MutableStateFlow(0) // private mutable state flow
 36â†’ *     val counter = _counter.asStateFlow() // publicly exposed as read-only state flow
 37â†’ *
 38â†’ *     fun inc() {
 39â†’ *         _counter.update { count -> count + 1 } // atomic, safe for concurrent use
 40â†’ *     }
 41â†’ * }
 42â†’ * ```
 43â†’ *
 44â†’ * Having two instances of the above `CounterModel` class one can define the sum of their counters like this:
 45â†’ *
 46â†’ * ```
 47â†’ * val aModel = CounterModel()
 48â†’ * val bModel = CounterModel()
 49â†’ * val sumFlow: Flow<Int> = aModel.counter.combine(bModel.counter) { a, b -> a + b }
 50â†’ * ```
 51â†’ *
 52â†’ * As an alternative to the above usage with the `MutableStateFlow(...)` constructor function,
 53â†’ * any _cold_ [Flow] can be converted to a state flow using the [stateIn] operator.
 54â†’ *
 55â†’ * ### Strong equality-based conflation
 56â†’ *
 57â†’ * Values in state flow are conflated using [Any.equals] comparison in a similar way to
 58â†’ * [distinctUntilChanged] operator. It is used to conflate incoming updates
 59â†’ * to [value][MutableStateFlow.value] in [MutableStateFlow] and to suppress emission of the values to collectors
 60â†’ * when new value is equal to the previously emitted one. State flow behavior with classes that violate
 61â†’ * the contract for [Any.equals] is unspecified.
 62â†’ *
 63â†’ * ### State flow is a shared flow
 64â†’ *
 65â†’ * State flow is a special-purpose, high-performance, and efficient implementation of [SharedFlow] for the narrow,
 66â†’ * but widely used case of sharing a state. See the [SharedFlow] documentation for the basic rules,
 67â†’ * constraints, and operators that are applicable to all shared flows.
 68â†’ *
 69â†’ * State flow always has an initial value, replays one most recent value to new subscribers, does not buffer any
 70â†’ * more values, but keeps the last emitted one, and does not support [resetReplayCache][MutableSharedFlow.resetReplayCache].
 71â†’ * A state flow behaves identically to a shared flow when it is created
 72â†’ * with the following parameters and the [distinctUntilChanged] operator is applied to it:
 73â†’ *
 74â†’ * ```
 75â†’ * // MutableStateFlow(initialValue) is a shared flow with the following parameters:
 76â†’ * val shared = MutableSharedFlow(
 77â†’ *     replay = 1,
 78â†’ *     onBufferOverflow = BufferOverflow.DROP_OLDEST
 79â†’ * )
 80â†’ * shared.tryEmit(initialValue) // emit the initial value
 81â†’ * val state = shared.distinctUntilChanged() // get StateFlow-like behavior
 82â†’ * ```
 83â†’ *
 84â†’ * Use [SharedFlow] when you need a [StateFlow] with tweaks in its behavior such as extra buffering, replaying more
 85â†’ * values, or omitting the initial value.
 86â†’ * 
 87â†’ * ### StateFlow vs ConflatedBroadcastChannel
 88â†’ *
 89â†’ * Conceptually, state flow is similar to [ConflatedBroadcastChannel]
 90â†’ * and is designed to completely replace it.
 91â†’ * It has the following important differences:
 92â†’ *
 93â†’ * * `StateFlow` is simpler, because it does not have to implement all the [Channel] APIs, which allows
 94â†’ *   for faster, garbage-free implementation, unlike `ConflatedBroadcastChannel` implementation that
 95â†’ *   allocates objects on each emitted value.
 96â†’ * * `StateFlow` always has a value which can be safely read at any time via [value] property.
 97â†’ *    Unlike `ConflatedBroadcastChannel`, there is no way to create a state flow without a value.
 98â†’ * * `StateFlow` has a clear separation into a read-only `StateFlow` interface and a [MutableStateFlow].
 99â†’ * * `StateFlow` conflation is based on equality like [distinctUntilChanged] operator,
100â†’ *    unlike conflation in `ConflatedBroadcastChannel` that is based on reference identity.
101â†’ * * `StateFlow` cannot be closed like `ConflatedBroadcastChannel` and can never represent a failure.
102â†’ *    All errors and completion signals should be explicitly _materialized_ if needed.
103â†’ *
104â†’ * `StateFlow` is designed to better cover typical use-cases of keeping track of state changes in time, taking
105â†’ * more pragmatic design choices for the sake of convenience.
106â†’ *
107â†’ * To migrate [ConflatedBroadcastChannel] usage to [StateFlow], start by replacing usages of the `ConflatedBroadcastChannel()`
108â†’ * constructor with `MutableStateFlow(initialValue)`, using `null` as an initial value if you don't have one.
109â†’ * Replace [send][ConflatedBroadcastChannel.send] and [trySend][ConflatedBroadcastChannel.trySend] calls
110â†’ * with updates to the state flow's [MutableStateFlow.value], and convert subscribers' code to flow operators.
111â†’ * You can use the [filterNotNull] operator to mimic behavior of a `ConflatedBroadcastChannel` without initial value.
112â†’ *
113â†’ * ### Concurrency
114â†’ *
115â†’ * All methods of state flow are **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
116â†’ * external synchronization.
117â†’ *
118â†’ * ### Operator fusion
119â†’ *
120â†’ * Application of [flowOn][Flow.flowOn], [conflate][Flow.conflate],
121â†’ * [buffer] with [CONFLATED][Channel.CONFLATED] or [RENDEZVOUS][Channel.RENDEZVOUS] capacity,
122â†’ * [distinctUntilChanged][Flow.distinctUntilChanged], or [cancellable] operators to a state flow has no effect.
123â†’ * 
124â†’ * ### Implementation notes
125â†’ *
126â†’ * State flow implementation is optimized for memory consumption and allocation-freedom. It uses a lock to ensure
127â†’ * thread-safety, but suspending collector coroutines are resumed outside of this lock to avoid dead-locks when
128â†’ * using unconfined coroutines. Adding new subscribers has `O(1)` amortized cost, but updating a [value] has `O(N)`
129â†’ * cost, where `N` is the number of active subscribers.
130â†’ *
131â†’ * ### Not stable for inheritance
132â†’ *
133â†’ * **`The StateFlow` interface is not stable for inheritance in 3rd party libraries**, as new methods
134â†’ * might be added to this interface in the future, but is stable for use.
135â†’ * Use the `MutableStateFlow(value)` constructor function to create an implementation.
136â†’ */
137â†’public interface StateFlow<out T> : SharedFlow<T> {
138â†’    /**
139â†’     * The current value of this state flow.
140â†’     */

142â†’}

144â†’/**
145â†’ * A mutable [StateFlow] that provides a setter for [value].
146â†’ * An instance of `MutableStateFlow` with the given initial `value` can be created using
147â†’ * `MutableStateFlow(value)` constructor function.
148â†’
149â†’ * See the [StateFlow] documentation for details on state flows.
150â†’ * Note that all emission-related operators, such as [value]'s setter, [emit], and [tryEmit], are conflated using [Any.equals].
151â†’ *
152â†’ * ### Not stable for inheritance
153â†’ *
154â†’ * **The `MutableStateFlow` interface is not stable for inheritance in 3rd party libraries**, as new methods
155â†’ * might be added to this interface in the future, but is stable for use.
156â†’ * Use the `MutableStateFlow()` constructor function to create an implementation.
157â†’ */
158â†’public interface MutableStateFlow<T> : StateFlow<T>, MutableSharedFlow<T> {
159â†’    /**
160â†’     * The current value of this state flow.
161â†’     *
162â†’     * Setting a value that is [equal][Any.equals] to the previous one does nothing.
163â†’     *
164â†’     * This property is **thread-safe** and can be safely updated from concurrent coroutines without
165â†’     * external synchronization.

167â†’    public override var value: T
168â†’
169â†’    /**
170â†’     * Atomically compares the current [value] with [expect] and sets it to [update] if it is equal to [expect].
171â†’     * The result is `true` if the [value] was set to [update] and `false` otherwise.
172â†’     *
173â†’     * This function use a regular comparison using [Any.equals]. If both [expect] and [update] are equal to the
174â†’     * current [value], this function returns `true`, but it does not actually change the reference that is
175â†’     * stored in the [value].
176â†’     *
177â†’     * This method is **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
178â†’     * external synchronization.
179â†’     */
180â†’    public fun compareAndSet(expect: T, update: T): Boolean
181â†’}

237â†’}
238â†’
239â†’// ------------------------------------ Implementation ------------------------------------
240â†’
241â†’private val NONE = Symbol("NONE")
242â†’
243â†’private val PENDING = Symbol("PENDING")
244â†’
245â†’// StateFlow slots are allocated for its collectors
246â†’private class StateFlowSlot : AbstractSharedFlowSlot<StateFlowImpl<*>>() {
247â†’    /**
248â†’     * Each slot can have one of the following states:
249â†’     *
250â†’     * * `null` -- it is not used right now. Can [allocateLocked] to new collector.
251â†’     * * `NONE` -- used by a collector, but neither suspended nor has pending value.
252â†’     * * `PENDING` -- pending to process new value.
253â†’     * * `CancellableContinuationImpl<Unit>` -- suspended waiting for new value.
254â†’     *
255â†’     * It is important that default `null` value is used, because there can be a race between allocation
256â†’     * of a new slot and trying to do [makePending] on this slot.
257â†’     */
258â†’    private val _state = atomic<Any?>(null)
259â†’
260â†’    override fun allocateLocked(flow: StateFlowImpl<*>): Boolean {
261â†’        // No need for atomic check & update here, since allocated happens under StateFlow lock
262â†’        if (_state.value != null) return false // not free
263â†’        _state.value = NONE // allocated
264â†’        return true
265â†’    }
266â†’
267â†’    override fun freeLocked(flow: StateFlowImpl<*>): Array<Continuation<Unit>?> {
268â†’        _state.value = null // free now
269â†’        return EMPTY_RESUMES // nothing more to do
270â†’    }
271â†’
272â†’    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
273â†’    fun makePending() {
274â†’        _state.loop { state ->
275â†’            when {
276â†’                state == null -> return // this slot is free - skip it
277â†’                state === PENDING -> return // already pending, nothing to do
278â†’                state === NONE -> { // mark as pending
279â†’                    if (_state.compareAndSet(state, PENDING)) return
280â†’                }
281â†’                else -> { // must be a suspend continuation state
282â†’                    // we must still use CAS here since continuation may get cancelled and free the slot at any time
283â†’                    if (_state.compareAndSet(state, NONE)) {
284â†’                        (state as CancellableContinuationImpl<Unit>).resume(Unit)
285â†’                        return
286â†’                    }
287â†’                }
288â†’            }
289â†’        }
290â†’    }
291â†’
292â†’    fun takePending(): Boolean = _state.getAndSet(NONE)!!.let { state ->
293â†’        assert { state !is CancellableContinuationImpl<*> }
294â†’        return state === PENDING
295â†’    }
296â†’
297â†’    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
298â†’    suspend fun awaitPending(): Unit = suspendCancellableCoroutine sc@ { cont ->
299â†’        assert { _state.value !is CancellableContinuationImpl<*> } // can be NONE or PENDING
300â†’        if (_state.compareAndSet(NONE, cont)) return@sc // installed continuation, waiting for pending
301â†’        // CAS failed -- the only possible reason is that it is already in pending state now
302â†’        assert { _state.value === PENDING }
303â†’        cont.resume(Unit)
304â†’    }
305â†’}
306â†’
307â†’private class StateFlowImpl<T>(
308â†’    initialState: Any // T | NULL
309â†’) : AbstractSharedFlow<StateFlowSlot>(), MutableStateFlow<T>, CancellableFlow<T>, FusibleFlow<T> {
310â†’    private val _state = atomic(initialState) // T | NULL
311â†’    private var sequence = 0 // serializes updates, value update is in process when sequence is odd
312â†’
313â†’    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
314â†’    public override var value: T
315â†’        get() = NULL.unbox(_state.value)
316â†’        set(value) { updateState(null, value ?: NULL) }
317â†’
318â†’    override fun compareAndSet(expect: T, update: T): Boolean =
319â†’        updateState(expect ?: NULL, update ?: NULL)
320â†’
321â†’    private fun updateState(expectedState: Any?, newState: Any): Boolean {
322â†’        var curSequence = 0
323â†’        var curSlots: Array<StateFlowSlot?>? = this.slots // benign race, we will not use it
324â†’        synchronized(this) {
325â†’            val oldState = _state.value
326â†’            if (expectedState != null && oldState != expectedState) return false // CAS support
327â†’            if (oldState == newState) return true // Don't do anything if value is not changing, but CAS -> true
328â†’            _state.value = newState
329â†’            curSequence = sequence
330â†’            if (curSequence and 1 == 0) { // even sequence means quiescent state flow (no ongoing update)
331â†’                curSequence++ // make it odd
332â†’                sequence = curSequence
333â†’            } else {
334â†’                // update is already in process, notify it, and return
335â†’                sequence = curSequence + 2 // change sequence to notify, keep it odd
336â†’                return true // updated
337â†’            }
338â†’            curSlots = slots // read current reference to collectors under lock
339â†’        }
340â†’        /*
341â†’           Fire value updates outside of the lock to avoid deadlocks with unconfined coroutines.
342â†’           Loop until we're done firing all the changes. This is a sort of simple flat combining that
343â†’           ensures sequential firing of concurrent updates and avoids the storm of collector resumes
344â†’           when updates happen concurrently from many threads.
345â†’         */
346â†’        while (true) {
347â†’            // Benign race on element read from array
348â†’            curSlots?.forEach {
349â†’                it?.makePending()
350â†’            }
351â†’            // check if the value was updated again while we were updating the old one
352â†’            synchronized(this) {
353â†’                if (sequence == curSequence) { // nothing changed, we are done
354â†’                    sequence = curSequence + 1 // make sequence even again
355â†’                    return true // done, updated
356â†’                }
357â†’                // reread everything for the next loop under the lock
358â†’                curSequence = sequence
359â†’                curSlots = slots
360â†’            }
361â†’        }
362â†’    }
363â†’
364â†’    override val replayCache: List<T>
365â†’        get() = listOf(value)
366â†’
367â†’    override fun tryEmit(value: T): Boolean {
368â†’        this.value = value
369â†’        return true
370â†’    }
371â†’
372â†’    override suspend fun emit(value: T) {
373â†’        this.value = value
374â†’    }
375â†’
376â†’    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
377â†’    override fun resetReplayCache() {
378â†’        throw UnsupportedOperationException("MutableStateFlow.resetReplayCache is not supported")
379â†’    }
380â†’
381â†’    override suspend fun collect(collector: FlowCollector<T>): Nothing {
382â†’        val slot = allocateSlot()
383â†’        try {
384â†’            if (collector is SubscribedFlowCollector) collector.onSubscription()
385â†’            val collectorJob = currentCoroutineContext()[Job]
386â†’            var oldState: Any? = null // previously emitted T!! | NULL (null -- nothing emitted yet)
387â†’            // The loop is arranged so that it starts delivering current value without waiting first
388â†’            while (true) {
389â†’                // Here the coroutine could have waited for a while to be dispatched,
390â†’                // so we use the most recent state here to ensure the best possible conflation of stale values
391â†’                val newState = _state.value
392â†’                // always check for cancellation
393â†’                collectorJob?.ensureActive()
394â†’                // Conflate value emissions using equality
395â†’                if (oldState == null || oldState != newState) {
396â†’                    collector.emit(NULL.unbox(newState))
397â†’                    oldState = newState
398â†’                }
399â†’                // Note: if awaitPending is cancelled, then it bails out of this loop and calls freeSlot
400â†’                if (!slot.takePending()) { // try fast-path without suspending first
401â†’                    slot.awaitPending() // only suspend for new values when needed
402â†’                }
403â†’            }
404â†’        } finally {
405â†’            freeSlot(slot)
406â†’        }
407â†’    }
408â†’
409â†’    override fun createSlot() = StateFlowSlot()
410â†’    override fun createSlotArray(size: Int): Array<StateFlowSlot?> = arrayOfNulls(size)
411â†’
412â†’    override fun fuse(context: CoroutineContext, capacity: Int, onBufferOverflow: BufferOverflow) =
413â†’        fuseStateFlow(context, capacity, onBufferOverflow)
414â†’}
415â†’
416â†’internal fun <T> StateFlow<T>.fuseStateFlow(
417â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
418â†’    capacity: Int,
419â†’    onBufferOverflow: BufferOverflow
420â†’): Flow<T> {
421â†’    // state flow is always conflated so additional conflation does not have any effect
422â†’    assert { capacity != Channel.CONFLATED } // should be desugared by callers
423â†’    if ((capacity in 0..1 || capacity == Channel.BUFFERED) && onBufferOverflow == BufferOverflow.DROP_OLDEST) {
424â†’        return this
425â†’    }
426â†’    return fuseSharedFlow(context, capacity, onBufferOverflow)
427â†’}

After analyzing the codebase and the description, here is the root cause and proposed targeted fix:

### Root Cause

- In version 1.6.4, `SharedFlow.toList(acc)` behaved as expected, collecting emitted items into the provided list (which acted identically to a normal Flow).
- In 1.7.0-Beta, `SharedFlow.toList` is still implemented as an extension operator:
  ```kotlin
  public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toList(destination: MutableList<T> = ArrayList()): List<T> =
      (this as Flow<T>).toList()
  ```
- However, this operator is now **deprecated** and emits a warning: "SharedFlow never completes, so this terminal operation never completes."
- Despite the warning, the implementation (delegating to `(this as Flow<T>).toList(destination)`) is correct. But there can be compatibility issues if new deprecation mechanisms, warnings, or behavior changes are causing trouble in user test code (e.g., breaking builds due to deprecation error level, or not actually collecting into `acc` due to operator changes).

### Targeted Fix

**Ensure SharedFlow.toList(destination) correctly delegates to Flow.toList(destination) and does not block usage.**
- The function should remain as a simple delegation to `Flow.toList(destination)`, and the signature should remain compatible with previous versions.
- If the deprecation warning is set to a higher level (like ERROR) or interferes with normal collection (early errors, etc.), lower to `DeprecationLevel.WARNING` to allow usage.
- **Key Point:** Keep the method so users can use `SharedFlow.toList(destination)` for testing, just as in 1.6.4.

### Code Change

The current implementation in `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt` (lines 147-149) is correct, but the warning/deprecation must be at most a WARNING (never ERROR).

**Review:**
```kotlin
@Deprecated(
    message = "SharedFlow never completes, so this terminal operation never completes.",
    level = DeprecationLevel.WARNING
)
@InlineOnly
public suspend inline fun <T> SharedFlow<T>.toList(destination: MutableList<T> = ArrayList()): List<T> =
    (this as Flow<T>).toList(destination)
```

- If the current level is ERROR, change it to WARNING.
- If it's already WARNING, ensure that is preserved and the signature is (destination: MutableList<T> = ArrayList()).

**If the existing code matches above, no code change is needed. If not, update.**

---

## Implementation Plan

1. **Verify/Update Lint.kt:** Ensure the `toList` extension for SharedFlow is as above (level=WARNING, signature + implementation as simple delegation).
2. **If not, perform targeted edit.**

---

Let's now check the **real current code** for exact match.

 14â†’/**
 15â†’ * A _hot_ [Flow] that shares emitted values among all its collectors in a broadcast fashion, so that all collectors
 16â†’ * get all emitted values. A shared flow is called _hot_ because its active instance exists independently of the
 17â†’ * presence of collectors. This is opposed to a regular [Flow], such as defined by the [`flow { ... }`][flow] function,
 18â†’ * which is _cold_ and is started separately for each collector.
 19â†’ *
 20â†’ * **Shared flow never completes**. A call to [Flow.collect] on a shared flow never completes normally, and
 21â†’ * neither does a coroutine started by the [Flow.launchIn] function. An active collector of a shared flow is called a _subscriber_.
 22â†’ *
 23â†’ * A subscriber of a shared flow can be cancelled. This usually happens when the scope in which the coroutine is running
 24â†’ * is cancelled. A subscriber to a shared flow is always [cancellable][Flow.cancellable], and checks for
 25â†’ * cancellation before each emission. Note that most terminal operators like [Flow.toList] would also not complete,
 26â†’ * when applied to a shared flow, but flow-truncating operators like [Flow.take] and [Flow.takeWhile] can be used on a
 27â†’ * shared flow to turn it into a completing one.
 28â†’ *
 29â†’ * A [mutable shared flow][MutableSharedFlow] is created using the [MutableSharedFlow(...)] constructor function.
 30â†’ * Its state can be updated by [emitting][MutableSharedFlow.emit] values to it and performing other operations.
 31â†’ * See the [MutableSharedFlow] documentation for details.
 32â†’ *
 33â†’ * [SharedFlow] is useful for broadcasting events that happen inside an application to subscribers that can come and go.
 34â†’ * For example, the following class encapsulates an event bus that distributes events to all subscribers
 35â†’ * in a _rendezvous_ manner, suspending until all subscribers receive emitted event:
 36â†’ *
 37â†’ * ```
 38â†’ * class EventBus {
 39â†’ *     private val _events = MutableSharedFlow<Event>() // private mutable shared flow
 40â†’ *     val events = _events.asSharedFlow() // publicly exposed as read-only shared flow
 41â†’ *
 42â†’ *     suspend fun produceEvent(event: Event) {
 43â†’ *         _events.emit(event) // suspends until all subscribers receive it
 44â†’ *     }
 45â†’ * }
 46â†’ * ```
 47â†’ *
 48â†’ * As an alternative to the above usage with the `MutableSharedFlow(...)` constructor function,
 49â†’ * any _cold_ [Flow] can be converted to a shared flow using the [shareIn] operator.
 50â†’ *
 51â†’ * There is a specialized implementation of shared flow for the case where the most recent state value needs
 52â†’ * to be shared. See [StateFlow] for details.
 53â†’ *
 54â†’ * ### Replay cache and buffer
 55â†’ *
 56â†’ * A shared flow keeps a specific number of the most recent values in its _replay cache_. Every new subscriber first
 57â†’ * gets the values from the replay cache and then gets new emitted values. The maximum size of the replay cache is
 58â†’ * specified when the shared flow is created by the `replay` parameter. A snapshot of the current replay cache
 59â†’ * is available via the [replayCache] property and it can be reset with the [MutableSharedFlow.resetReplayCache] function.
 60â†’ *
 61â†’ * A replay cache also provides buffer for emissions to the shared flow, allowing slow subscribers to
 62â†’ * get values from the buffer without suspending emitters. The buffer space determines how much slow subscribers
 63â†’ * can lag from the fast ones. When creating a shared flow, additional buffer capacity beyond replay can be reserved
 64â†’ * using the `extraBufferCapacity` parameter.
 65â†’ * 
 66â†’ * A shared flow with a buffer can be configured to avoid suspension of emitters on buffer overflow using
 67â†’ * the `onBufferOverflow` parameter, which is equal to one of the entries of the [BufferOverflow] enum. When a strategy other
 68â†’ * than [SUSPENDED][BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] is configured, emissions to the shared flow never suspend.
 69â†’ *
 70â†’ * **Buffer overflow condition can happen only when there is at least one subscriber that is not ready to accept
 71â†’ * the new value.**  In the absence of subscribers only the most recent `replay` values are stored and the buffer
 72â†’ * overflow behavior is never triggered and has no effect. In particular, in the absence of subscribers emitter never
 73â†’ * suspends despite [BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] option and [BufferOverflow.DROP_LATEST] option does not have effect either.
 74â†’ * Essentially, the behavior in the absence of subscribers is always similar to [BufferOverflow.DROP_OLDEST],
 75â†’ * but the buffer is just of `replay` size (without any `extraBufferCapacity`).
 76â†’ *
 77â†’ * ### Unbuffered shared flow
 78â†’ *
 79â†’ * A default implementation of a shared flow that is created with `MutableSharedFlow()` constructor function
 80â†’ * without parameters has no replay cache nor additional buffer.
 81â†’ * [emit][MutableSharedFlow.emit] call to such a shared flow suspends until all subscribers receive the emitted value
 82â†’ * and returns immediately if there are no subscribers.
 83â†’ * Thus, [tryEmit][MutableSharedFlow.tryEmit] call succeeds and returns `true` only if
 84â†’ * there are no subscribers (in which case the emitted value is immediately lost).
 85â†’ *
 86â†’ * ### SharedFlow vs BroadcastChannel
 87â†’ *
 88â†’ * Conceptually shared flow is similar to [BroadcastChannel][BroadcastChannel]
 89â†’ * and is designed to completely replace it.
 90â†’ * It has the following important differences:
 91â†’ *
 92â†’ * * `SharedFlow` is simpler, because it does not have to implement all the [Channel] APIs, which allows
 93â†’ *    for faster and simpler implementation.
 94â†’ * * `SharedFlow` supports configurable replay and buffer overflow strategy.
 95â†’ * * `SharedFlow` has a clear separation into a read-only `SharedFlow` interface and a [MutableSharedFlow].
 96â†’ * * `SharedFlow` cannot be closed like `BroadcastChannel` and can never represent a failure.
 97â†’ *    All errors and completion signals should be explicitly _materialized_ if needed.
 98â†’ *
 99â†’ * To migrate [BroadcastChannel] usage to [SharedFlow], start by replacing usages of the `BroadcastChannel(capacity)`
100â†’ * constructor with `MutableSharedFlow(0, extraBufferCapacity=capacity)` (broadcast channel does not replay
101â†’ * values to new subscribers). Replace [send][BroadcastChannel.send] and [trySend][BroadcastChannel.trySend] calls
102â†’ * with [emit][MutableStateFlow.emit] and [tryEmit][MutableStateFlow.tryEmit], and convert subscribers' code to flow operators.
103â†’ *
104â†’ * ### Concurrency
105â†’ *
106â†’ * All methods of shared flow are **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
107â†’ * external synchronization.
108â†’ *
109â†’ * ### Operator fusion
110â†’ *
111â†’ * Application of [flowOn][Flow.flowOn], [buffer] with [RENDEZVOUS][Channel.RENDEZVOUS] capacity,
112â†’ * or [cancellable] operators to a shared flow has no effect.
113â†’ *
114â†’ * ### Implementation notes
115â†’ *
116â†’ * Shared flow implementation uses a lock to ensure thread-safety, but suspending collector and emitter coroutines are
117â†’ * resumed outside of this lock to avoid deadlocks when using unconfined coroutines. Adding new subscribers
118â†’ * has `O(1)` amortized cost, but emitting has `O(N)` cost, where `N` is the number of subscribers.
119â†’ *
120â†’ * ### Not stable for inheritance
121â†’ *
122â†’ * **The `SharedFlow` interface is not stable for inheritance in 3rd party libraries**, as new methods
123â†’ * might be added to this interface in the future, but is stable for use.
124â†’ * Use the `MutableSharedFlow(replay, ...)` constructor function to create an implementation.
125â†’ */
126â†’public interface SharedFlow<out T> : Flow<T> {
127â†’    /**
128â†’     * A snapshot of the replay cache.
129â†’     */
130â†’    public val replayCache: List<T>
131â†’
132â†’    /**
133â†’     * Accepts the given [collector] and [emits][FlowCollector.emit] values into it.
134â†’     * To emit values from a shared flow into a specific collector, either `collector.emitAll(flow)` or `collect { ... }`
135â†’     * SAM-conversion can be used.
136â†’     *
137â†’     * **A shared flow never completes**. A call to [Flow.collect] or any other terminal operator
138â†’     * on a shared flow never completes normally.
139â†’     *
140â†’     * @see [Flow.collect] for implementation and inheritance details.
141â†’     */
142â†’    override suspend fun collect(collector: FlowCollector<T>): Nothing
143â†’}
144â†’
145â†’/**
146â†’ * A mutable [SharedFlow] that provides functions to [emit] values to the flow.
147â†’ * An instance of `MutableSharedFlow` with the given configuration parameters can be created using `MutableSharedFlow(...)`
148â†’ * constructor function.
149â†’ *
150â†’ * See the [SharedFlow] documentation for details on shared flows.
151â†’ *
152â†’ * `MutableSharedFlow` is a [SharedFlow] that also provides the abilities to [emit] a value,
153â†’ * to [tryEmit] without suspension if possible, to track the [subscriptionCount],
154â†’ * and to [resetReplayCache].
155â†’ *
156â†’ * ### Concurrency
157â†’ *
158â†’ * All methods of shared flow are **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
159â†’ * external synchronization.
160â†’ *
161â†’ * ### Not stable for inheritance
162â†’ *
163â†’ * **The `MutableSharedFlow` interface is not stable for inheritance in 3rd party libraries**, as new methods
164â†’ * might be added to this interface in the future, but is stable for use.
165â†’ * Use the `MutableSharedFlow(...)` constructor function to create an implementation.
166â†’ */
167â†’public interface MutableSharedFlow<T> : SharedFlow<T>, FlowCollector<T> {
168â†’    /**
169â†’     * Emits a [value] to this shared flow, suspending on buffer overflow.
170â†’     *
171â†’     * This call can suspend only when the [BufferOverflow] strategy is
172â†’     * [SUSPEND][BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] **and** there are subscribers collecting this shared flow.
173â†’     *
174â†’     * If there are no subscribers, the buffer is not used.
175â†’     * Instead, the most recently emitted value is simply stored into
176â†’     * the replay cache if one was configured, displacing the older elements there,
177â†’     * or dropped if no replay cache was configured.
178â†’     *
179â†’     * See [tryEmit] for a non-suspending variant of this function.
180â†’     *
181â†’     * This method is **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
182â†’     * external synchronization.
183â†’     */
184â†’    override suspend fun emit(value: T)
185â†’
186â†’    /**
187â†’     * Tries to emit a [value] to this shared flow without suspending. It returns `true` if the value was
188â†’     * emitted successfully (see below). When this function returns `false`, it means that a call to a plain [emit]
189â†’     * function would suspend until there is buffer space available.
190â†’     *
191â†’     * This call can return `false` only when the [BufferOverflow] strategy is
192â†’     * [SUSPEND][BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] **and** there are subscribers collecting this shared flow.
193â†’     *
194â†’     * If there are no subscribers, the buffer is not used.
195â†’     * Instead, the most recently emitted value is simply stored into
196â†’     * the replay cache if one was configured, displacing the older elements there,
197â†’     * or dropped if no replay cache was configured. In any case, `tryEmit` returns `true`.
198â†’     *
199â†’     * This method is **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
200â†’     * external synchronization.
201â†’     */
202â†’    public fun tryEmit(value: T): Boolean
203â†’
204â†’    /**
205â†’     * The number of subscribers (active collectors) to this shared flow.
206â†’     *
207â†’     * The integer in the resulting [StateFlow] is not negative and starts with zero for a freshly created
208â†’     * shared flow.
209â†’     *
210â†’     * This state can be used to react to changes in the number of subscriptions to this shared flow.
211â†’     * For example, if you need to call `onActive` when the first subscriber appears and `onInactive`
212â†’     * when the last one disappears, you can set it up like this:
213â†’     *
214â†’     * ```
215â†’     * sharedFlow.subscriptionCount
216â†’     *     .map { count -> count > 0 } // map count into active/inactive flag
217â†’     *     .distinctUntilChanged() // only react to true<->false changes
218â†’     *     .onEach { isActive -> // configure an action
219â†’     *         if (isActive) onActive() else onInactive()
220â†’     *     }
221â†’     *     .launchIn(scope) // launch it
222â†’     * ```
223â†’     *
224â†’     * Implementation note: the resulting flow **does not** conflate subscription count.
225â†’     */
226â†’    public val subscriptionCount: StateFlow<Int>
227â†’
228â†’    /**
229â†’     * Resets the [replayCache] of this shared flow to an empty state.
230â†’     * New subscribers will be receiving only the values that were emitted after this call,
231â†’     * while old subscribers will still be receiving previously buffered values.
232â†’     * To reset a shared flow to an initial value, emit the value after this call.
233â†’     *
234â†’     * On a [MutableStateFlow], which always contains a single value, this function is not
235â†’     * supported, and throws an [UnsupportedOperationException]. To reset a [MutableStateFlow]
236â†’     * to an initial value, just update its [value][MutableStateFlow.value].
237â†’     *
238â†’     * This method is **thread-safe** and can be safely invoked from concurrent coroutines without
239â†’     * external synchronization.
240â†’     *
241â†’     * **Note: This is an experimental api.** This function may be removed or renamed in the future.
242â†’     */
243â†’    @ExperimentalCoroutinesApi
244â†’    public fun resetReplayCache()
245â†’}

247â†’/**
248â†’ * Creates a [MutableSharedFlow] with the given configuration parameters.
249â†’ *
250â†’ * This function throws [IllegalArgumentException] on unsupported values of parameters or combinations thereof.
251â†’ *
252â†’ * @param replay the number of values replayed to new subscribers (cannot be negative, defaults to zero).
253â†’ * @param extraBufferCapacity the number of values buffered in addition to `replay`.
254â†’ *   [emit][MutableSharedFlow.emit] does not suspend while there is a buffer space remaining (optional, cannot be negative, defaults to zero).
255â†’ * @param onBufferOverflow configures an [emit][MutableSharedFlow.emit] action on buffer overflow. Optional, defaults to
256â†’ *   [suspending][BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] attempts to emit a value.
257â†’ *   Values other than [BufferOverflow.SUSPEND] are supported only when `replay > 0` or `extraBufferCapacity > 0`.
258â†’ *   **Buffer overflow can happen only when there is at least one subscriber that is not ready to accept
259â†’ *   the new value.** In the absence of subscribers only the most recent [replay] values are stored and
260â†’ *   the buffer overflow behavior is never triggered and has no effect.
261â†’ */
262â†’@Suppress("FunctionName", "UNCHECKED_CAST")
263â†’public fun <T> MutableSharedFlow(
264â†’    replay: Int = 0,
265â†’    extraBufferCapacity: Int = 0,
266â†’    onBufferOverflow: BufferOverflow = BufferOverflow.SUSPEND
267â†’): MutableSharedFlow<T> {
268â†’    require(replay >= 0) { "replay cannot be negative, but was $replay" }
269â†’    require(extraBufferCapacity >= 0) { "extraBufferCapacity cannot be negative, but was $extraBufferCapacity" }
270â†’    require(replay > 0 || extraBufferCapacity > 0 || onBufferOverflow == BufferOverflow.SUSPEND) {
271â†’        "replay or extraBufferCapacity must be positive with non-default onBufferOverflow strategy $onBufferOverflow"
272â†’    }
273â†’    val bufferCapacity0 = replay + extraBufferCapacity
274â†’    val bufferCapacity = if (bufferCapacity0 < 0) Int.MAX_VALUE else bufferCapacity0 // coerce to MAX_VALUE on overflow
275â†’    return SharedFlowImpl(replay, bufferCapacity, onBufferOverflow)
276â†’}

280â†’internal class SharedFlowSlot : AbstractSharedFlowSlot<SharedFlowImpl<*>>() {
281â†’    @JvmField
282â†’    var index = -1L // current "to-be-emitted" index, -1 means the slot is free now
283â†’
284â†’    @JvmField
285â†’    var cont: Continuation<Unit>? = null // collector waiting for new value
286â†’
287â†’    override fun allocateLocked(flow: SharedFlowImpl<*>): Boolean {
288â†’        if (index >= 0) return false // not free
289â†’        index = flow.updateNewCollectorIndexLocked()
290â†’        return true
291â†’    }
292â†’
293â†’    override fun freeLocked(flow: SharedFlowImpl<*>): Array<Continuation<Unit>?> {
294â†’        assert { index >= 0 }
295â†’        val oldIndex = index
296â†’        index = -1L
297â†’        cont = null // cleanup continuation reference
298â†’        return flow.updateCollectorIndexLocked(oldIndex)
299â†’    }
300â†’}

302â†’internal open class SharedFlowImpl<T>(
303â†’    private val replay: Int,
304â†’    private val bufferCapacity: Int,
305â†’    private val onBufferOverflow: BufferOverflow
306â†’) : AbstractSharedFlow<SharedFlowSlot>(), MutableSharedFlow<T>, CancellableFlow<T>, FusibleFlow<T> {
307â†’    /*
308â†’        Logical structure of the buffer
309â†’
310â†’                  buffered values
311â†’             /-----------------------\
312â†’                          replayCache      queued emitters
313â†’                          /----------\/----------------------\
314â†’         +---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+
315â†’         |   | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 | E | E | E | E | E | E |   |   |   |
316â†’         +---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+
317â†’               ^           ^           ^                      ^
318â†’               |           |           |                      |
319â†’              head         |      head + bufferSize     head + totalSize
320â†’               |           |           |
321â†’     index of the slowest  |    index of the fastest
322â†’      possible collector   |     possible collector
323â†’               |           |
324â†’               |     replayIndex == new collector's index
325â†’               \---------------------- /
